description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the gitlab/dind Docker image and define two stages: build and deploy. In the build stage add a job named build that runs the following commands: echo "## Setting up stack", apt-get update, curl -sSL https://get.haskellstack.org/ | sh, echo "## Running tests", stack test, echo "## Copying executable", stack install, and stores the resulting bin/ directory as an artifact. In the deploy stage add a job named build_image that depends on the build job, logs in to the GitLab Container Registry with docker login -u gitlab-ci-token -p $CI_JOB_TOKEN registry.gitlab.com, attempts to pull $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest || true, builds a Docker image with docker build --tag $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA --tag $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest ., and pushes both tags with docker push $CONTAINER_IMAGE:$CI_COMMIT_SHA and docker push $CONTAINER_IMAGE:latest. Use the variable CONTAINER_IMAGE set to registry.gitlab.com/$CI_PROJECT_PATH for image naming. The pipeline has no additional rules, caches, or services.
